growth_records.strain: C5 (isolated from nitrate-contaminated groundwater, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, TN)
growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: grown with 8 mM lactate as carbon source and electron donor and 3 ml of sterile air injected ... we were able to calculate a specific growth rate of 0.4 ± 0.02 h−1 and a generation time of 1.73 ± 0.09 h (Figure 4B Inset)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on lactate+O2 minimal medium, 30C, 225 rpm, pH 7.2 (bicarbonate-buffered). Reported mu=0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1; doubling_time 1.73 +/- 0.09 h (reported); consistent with ln2/0.4=1.733 h. Medium minimal salts listed; nitrate not present in this O2-fed condition. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Full lactate/nitrate base medium recipe given in Methods (trace elements/vitamins per Yoon 2013 and Hillesland 2014, not itemized here).

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: anaerobic versus microaerobic conditions, which were also found to have slightly different growth rates (Figure 5A; microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, microaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.2 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.47 CALCULATED = ln2/0.2 = 0.6931/0.2 = 3.466 h (not reported). No uptake/secretion rates. Lactate concentration not restated for this condition (8 mM used in the O2 condition); nitrate present as alternative electron acceptor.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, anaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.18 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.85 CALCULATED = ln2/0.18 = 0.6931/0.18 = 3.851 h (not reported). Nitrate-reducing (denitrification + respiratory ammonification). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures growing in complex media (LB) under microaerobic conditions exhibited highly reproducible growth rates (μ = 0.24 ± 0.004 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on complex LB medium, microaerobic, 30C, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.24 +/- 0.004 h-1. doubling_time_h=2.89 CALCULATED = ln2/0.24 = 0.6931/0.24 = 2.888 h (not reported). LB composition not itemized (named only). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.strain: C5 (isolated from groundwater well GW247, Oak Ridge, TN)
growth_records.medium: Aerobic minimal medium, 8 mM lactate + 1.5 mM ammonium (N-source)

up.units: mM (8 mM provided; specific uptake rate not reported)

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: when grown on 8 mM lactate, O2, and 1.5 mM ammonium as N-source, I. calvum displayed a typical growth curve with a specific growth rate of 0.4 ± 0.02 µ (1.7 ± 0.1 doublings/hour)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 (Gram-positive Actinobacterium), aerobic growth on 8 mM lactate with 1.5 mM ammonium as sole N-source (no aerobic growth on nitrate as N-source). Reported specific growth rate µ=0.4/h. Doubling time calculated td=ln2/µ=0.693/0.4=1.73 h. NOTE internal inconsistency in paper: '0.4 µ' stated as '1.7 doublings/hour' whereas µ=0.4/h implies 0.4/ln2=0.58 doublings/h; reported µ=0.4 retained. No specific uptake/secretion rate quantified for this condition.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic nitrate-reducing minimal medium, 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrate (ammonium-free state transition)

up.units: mM (12 mM provided; reduced via NarG to nitrite then split between NrfA/NirK)

sec.method: ion chromatography (CS-16 cation column); respiratory ammonification product

mfa.note: 13 µmol NH4+ (ammonification) + 24 µmol N2O-N (denitrification); cell counts rose 29% (3.5x, ~1.8 generations, net 5.4e5 cells/mL) despite no net ammonium in medium, indicating consumption of NrfA-produced ammonium

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: Net ammonium production was 13 ± 2.7 µmoles with the remainder of dissimilated N being used by the denitrification pathway (24 ± 4.2 µmoles N2O-N), resulting in a recovery of 97.4% dissimilated N.
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 anaerobic batch, 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrate, ammonium-free state-transition test of Nrf functionality. Products reported as total µmoles over 48 h (NOT per-biomass rates): net NH4+ 13±2.7 µmol, N2O-N 24±4.2 µmol; 97.4% dissimilated-N recovery. Growth: +29% cell counts (~1.8 generations); µ not numerically reported for this condition. Minor acetate (100-200 µM range) noted elsewhere in paper from incomplete lactate oxidation (transcriptomics condition, 8 mM lactate/12 mM nitrate).

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic minimal medium, 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrite (nitrite as sole electron acceptor, C:acceptor ratio = 2)

up.units: mM (12 mM provided as sole acceptor)

mfa.note: With nitrite as sole acceptor, denitrification (33.4 µmol N2O-N) exceeds ammonification (8.0 µmol NH4+); no immediate growth, contrasting nitrate condition where growth is coupled to nitrate (not nitrite) reduction

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: When cultures were given nitrite, instead of nitrate as a terminal electron acceptor (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrite; ratio = 2), we observed no immediate growth ... measured more N2O than ammonium production (33.4 ± 4.8 µmoles N2O-N and 8.0 ±
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 anaerobic batch, 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrite (sole acceptor, C:acceptor=2). Products total µmoles (not per-biomass rates): N2O-N 33.4±4.8, NH4+ 8.0±2.5. No immediate growth; growth in this organism is coupled to nitrate (not nitrite) reduction, so µ/uptake rates not reported for this condition.
